title: Optional Chaining ?.
subtitle: Safe property access
goal: Learn to safely access object properties using ?.
explanation: The ?. operator is called Optional Chaining.
It lets you access properties safely.
If the object exists, the property is read.
If the object is null or undefined, no error is thrown.
Instead of an error, it returns undefined.

analogy.title: Analogy
analogy.text: You want to look inside a box. If the box exists, you look inside. If it does not, you simply get the answer “there is nothing here” without a crash.

remember: If the object is missing, it returns undefined.
?. is often used when working with APIs and user data.

mistake.title: Common mistake
mistake.text: Beginners access user.name even when user may be null. This causes an error. ?. solves this problem.

quiz.question: What does user?.name return when user = null?
